scope: Linux sandbox launcher binary for AgenC runtime subprocess isolation

requiredBehaviors: parses sandbox cwd, command cwd, serialized permission profile, hidden hardening flags, and command separator
rejects missing command and malformed permission profile input
uses execve for the non-proxy inner command stage
preserves final command argv0 while reserving agenc-linux-sandbox argv0 for helper reentry
relays parent signals to the outer bubblewrap child
edgeCases: command arguments after -- are preserved byte-for-byte as argv strings
inner-stage seccomp flag cannot be combined with legacy Landlock mode

requiredBehaviors: finds trusted system bubblewrap executables on PATH
probes --argv0 support with a child process
spawns the selected bubblewrap binary with array argv and inherited seccomp FD
edgeCases: cwd-local bwrap candidates are ignored to avoid workspace-controlled launcher selection
seccomp FD is kept open for the child and cleaned after exit

requiredBehaviors: full-write full-network commands can run without bubblewrap
network-isolated full-write commands retain full filesystem access under bubblewrap
restricted policies add namespace, proc, readonly, writable, unreadable, and protected metadata mounts
unreadable glob masks support star, question-mark, and character-class patterns
platform default readonly roots include common Linux and Nix system locations
edgeCases: missing protected metadata paths are represented as readonly empty directories or monitored when a parent repository metadata path must remain visible
protected metadata creation causes an otherwise successful launch to fail
unreadable glob matches are expanded and masked before launch
root-level unreadable glob scans fail closed instead of walking the filesystem root

requiredBehaviors: builds a cBPF seccomp program for restricted network mode
denies network syscalls while allowing AF_UNIX sockets in restricted mode
passes the generated program to bubblewrap with --seccomp FD
edgeCases: unsupported CPU architectures fail closed during filter generation
managed proxy mode computes a proxy-routed socket-family policy without blocking namespace listener setup

requiredBehaviors: recognizes the donor proxy environment key set case-insensitively
accepts only loopback proxy endpoints
rewrites proxy URLs to local loopback ports while preserving no-path formatting
prepares host-side UDS routes for loopback proxies
validates serialized route specs before opening namespace sockets
activates namespace-local loopback proxy listeners from the serialized route spec
runs managed proxy commands through an inner proxy-routed seccomp bubblewrap wrapper
edgeCases: non-loopback proxy endpoints are ignored by the route plan
missing proxy configuration fails closed for managed proxy mode
route activation rewrites only the proxy variables present in the serialized spec
active TCP and UDS proxy sockets are destroyed when route cleanup runs
duplicate proxy endpoints share a host-side UDS route
socks4 and socks4a proxy URLs default to port 1080
